_comment_about_this_file: Real-world forge-loop config used to run an hourly autonomous-fix
loop on openptxas (a CUDA PTX→SASS compiler) on a Windows machine.
1. pre-cycle: SSH to a Linux 'mower' host, snapshot its probe DB
2. source[scan-probes]: parse the snapshot, file gpu_incorrect
3. dispatch: claude --print with a 25-min timeout per session,
4. verifier: run the project's regression gate (probe-commit)
Substitute your paths and you have a working autofix loop for any
compiler that has a SQLite-style bug database.

prompt_kwargs.project_intro: You are fixing a bug in openptxas, a CUDA PTX→SASS compiler targeting NVIDIA SM_120 (Blackwell, RTX 5090).  Your job is to read the bug context, edit the source tree, validate, and commit.
likely_files: sass/pipeline.py — post-isel passes (FG29, FG32, FG56b, etc.)
validation_commands: workbench probe-commit --openptxas-repo C:/Users/kraken/openptxas --probe-dir C:/Users/kraken/_harvest/edges_db --resolves {eid} --corpus-gate-dir C:/Users/kraken/forge/analysis/vortex_ntt --message '<short fix description>' --push
hard_constraints: DO NOT modify the regression probe, the validation harness, or any test that's expected to pass.
DO NOT pass --no-test-suite or --skip-tests to probe-commit. The pytest gate is non-negotiable.
DO NOT bypass the post-commit hook (no --no-verify, no PROBE_RESOLVE_SKIP=1).
DO NOT amend or rebase prior commits; let probe-commit make a new commit on top.
DO NOT add narrating comments to the fix code. The commit message carries the explanation.
Keep the change minimal — surgical fixes age better than refactors.
prompt_kwargs.done_criterion: probe-commit exits 0 and the push lands on main. The post-commit hook will auto-resolve; the live-resolve loop in any running soak detects the HEAD change at its next 250-probe poll and re-verifies.
